der should be four. We have then to add three Vighatis or
deduct six Vighatis. The birth then must be at gh 10.58 or at
gh 10.49.
The second method has to be applied to determine when
the time thus rectified will hold good of a male or a female
birth.
Convert the duration between the local meantime of sun
rise and the local time of birth into Vighatis. Divide this by
225 and take the remainder. Divide the remainder into groups
of 15, 30, 45, 60 and 75 Vighatikas. The second and the fourth
groups represent a female birth. The rest indicate a male
birth.
0 Vighatis to 15 Vgh male
16 Vighatis to 45 Vgh female
46 Vighatis to 90 Vgh male
91 Vighatis to 150 Vgh female
151 Vighatis to 225 Vgh male
In this division out of225 Vighatis, 135 are given to male
births and 90 to female births. A closer view will show that
after 75 Vighatis at the end, the next fifteen also will cover
male births. This is paradoxical.
A person was born at 10 Gh. 58 Vigh after sunrise. The
